S.text: New patient, comprehensive exam. Chief complaint: difficulty reading small print, worsening over the past year.
Age 52; must hold reading material at arm's length; difficulty with restaurant menus. Denies distance blur.
Reports afternoon grittiness, worse with computer use.
Medical history: hypertension on lisinopril; no diabetes.

O.text: Pupils round and reactive, no relative afferent pupillary defect.
Manifest refraction OD plano -0.50 x 085; OS plano -0.50 x 095; distance VA sc 20/20 OU.
Near add +2.25 OU bringing near VA to 20/20.
Slit lamp: reduced tear breakup time ~6 s with punctate corneal staining OU; anterior chambers deep; very early nuclear lens changes not visually significant.
Dilated fundus exam: C/D 0.4 OU symmetric with healthy rims; maculae flat without age-related changes; peripheral retina attached and intact OU.

A.text: Dry eye disease OU (reduced TBUT, punctate keratopathy).
Very early nuclear lens changes OU, visually insignificant.

P.text: Prescribe +2.25 near add; progressive lenses recommended given lifestyle.
Begin artificial tears QID for dry eye; recheck ocular surface.
RTC 1 year, or sooner if dryness not controlled with drops.
